title: collea calcarata
intro: Collea calcarata, also known as the Indian truffle, is a type of plant that belongs to the family of Ascomycota, a group of fungi that grow underground in the form of truffles. The medicinal properties of Collea calcarata include its use in treating various health issues such as fever, rheumatism, and skin infections due to its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Collea calcarata include its ability to boost the immune system, reduce stress, and improve overall well-being. Collea calcarata is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and capsules, and can be found in various forms in traditional medicine, particularly in Ayurvedic and Unani practices.
